name: jira-cli
description: Interact with Jira from the command line to create, list, view, edit, and transition issues, manage sprints and epics, and perform common Jira workflows. Use when the user asks about Jira tasks, tickets, issues, sprints, or needs to manage project work items.
capabilities: Create, list, view, edit, and delete Jira issues
Generate plain text, JSON, or CSV output
author: Code-and-Sorts
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing SKILL.md with example commands for the legitimate jira-cli tool. The static analyzer flagged 99 patterns as potential issues, but ALL findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The 'findings' are standard bash syntax examples in documentation code blocks, not executable code. No command execution, network calls, or file system operations occur - this skill only provides documentation for a trusted open-source tool (github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli).
valueStatement: Manage Jira issues without leaving your terminal. This skill provides complete command-line documentation for creating, editing, tracking, and transitioning Jira issues, sprints, and epics.
userTitle: Manage Jira Issues from Command Line
limitations: Requires separate installation of jira-cli tool
Requires initial configuration with jira init command
Some features vary between Jira Cloud and Jira Server

useCases.title: Quick issue updates
useCases.description: Create and update Jira issues without switching to a browser or leaving your IDE

useCases.title: Sprint tracking
useCases.description: View sprint progress, list assigned issues, and manage team workloads from terminal

useCases.title: CI/CD integration
useCases.description: Update Jira issues automatically from deployment scripts and pipeline workflows

promptTemplates.title: Create a bug
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a high priority bug titled 'Login fails on mobile' with description 'Users cannot authenticate on iOS devices'

promptTemplates.title: List my tasks
promptTemplates.prompt: Show all Jira issues assigned to me that are in progress or to do status

promptTemplates.title: Complete an issue
promptTemplates.prompt: Move issue PROJ-123 to Done status and log 2 hours of work with comment 'Fixed authentication bug'

promptTemplates.title: Sprint overview
promptTemplates.prompt: List all issues in the current sprint and show their status in table format

bestPractices: Run jira init first to configure project settings and avoid repeated prompts
Use --plain flag for scripting to get predictable, machine-parseable output
Store JIRA_API_TOKEN securely in your shell profile or environment manager
antiPatterns: Never share or commit your JIRA_API_TOKEN to version control systems
Avoid interactive mode in automated scripts - use explicit flags instead
Do not rely on implicit project context - specify project explicitly in commands

faq.question: What Jira versions are supported?
faq.answer: Works with Jira Cloud and most Jira Server instances. Some advanced features may vary between versions.

faq.question: How do I install jira-cli?
faq.answer: Use brew install ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/jira-cli on macOS, or download binaries from the GitHub releases page.

faq.question: Can I use this in CI/CD pipelines?
faq.answer: Yes, use --plain or --raw flags for script-friendly output that integrates with automation tools.

faq.question: Is my Jira data secure with this tool?
faq.answer: Yes, jira-cli uses HTTPS for all API calls and your API token for authentication. This skill only provides documentation.

faq.question: What output formats are available?
faq.answer: Default interactive UI, --plain for text, --raw for JSON, and --csv for spreadsheet-compatible output.

faq.question: How do I manage sprints from command line?
faq.answer: Use jira sprint list --current to view active sprint, then jira sprint add to add issues to the sprint.
